title: Quantum Realizability of Probabilistic Theories Stable under Teleportation
abstract: The classification of general probabilistic theories (GPTs) whose CHSH value is stable under arbitrary rounds of teleportation and entanglement swapping was recently obtained in Dmello and Gross work and consists of seven families, indexed by characters of the Klein four-group $K_4$, the cyclic group $\mathbb{Z}_4$, and the dihedral group $D_4$. The question of which of these families admits a realization within standard quantum mechanics was left open. In this work we resolve this question completely. Using elementary representation theory, we prove that exactly two families are quantum-realizable, namely $χ^{K_4}_{1234}$ and $χ^{D_4}_{125}$, while the remaining five admit no quantum realization.
summary: This paper resolves the question of which teleportation-stable General Probabilistic Theories are compatible with standard quantum mechanics. By using group representation theory, the author proves that only two out of seven previously identified families are quantum-realizable. The study provides explicit constructions for these two families using Bell-basis measurements and POVMs.
main_problem: The paper aims to determine which of the seven families of General Probabilistic Theories (GPTs) that are stable under teleportation and entanglement swapping can be physically realized within the framework of standard quantum mechanics.
main_result: The author proves that exactly two of the seven families are quantum-realizable (specifically χ^{K4}_{1234} and χ^{D4}_{125}), while the remaining five families admit no quantum realization.
method: The study employs elementary representation theory, character tables of finite groups (K4, Z4, and D4), and the analysis of projective representations via the Schur cover (D8) to check for compatibility with unitary representations.
model_or_system: The mathematical framework involves General Probabilistic Theories (GPTs) and their operator spaces, specifically analyzing the action of correction groups (K4, Z4, D4) on the algebra of operators in finite-dimensional Hilbert spaces.
key_observables: CHSH value, conjugation character (χ_conj), and the multiplicity of irreducible representations.
important_parameters: Dimensions of the Hilbert space and the character components of the group representations.
main_assumptions: The analysis assumes finite-dimensional Hilbert spaces and focuses on the effective GPT classification provided by previous work.
figures_summary: Table I details the conjugacy classes of the dihedral group D4, and Table II summarizes the dimensions and character compositions of the seven GPT families.
paper_structure: The paper begins by defining the problem of quantum realizability, uses representation theory to establish constraints on the multiplicity of characters, proves the impossibility of five families through dimension and multiplicity obstructions, and concludes by providing explicit quantum constructions for the two realizable families.
why_interesting: This work provides fundamental constraints on the types of probabilistic theories that can exist as quantum-mechanical systems, which is relevant for understanding the boundaries of quantum information protocols like teleportation.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
